message:
fix(widgets/art): store terminal key on inner nodes for prefix scan

The command palette's `AdaptiveRadixTree` allows a value to live
directly on an `ArtNode::Inner` node when an inserted key happens
to end exactly at a compressed inner-node boundary (i.e. the key is
a strict prefix of another key). The previous `prefix_scan` and
`collect_all_inner` walkers intentionally dropped these inner
values because the tree didn't carry enough information to
reconstruct the full key without walking from the root:

    // Note: we can't emit the value here since we don't have the
    // full key. Values at inner nodes are only reachable via exact
    // lookup. For prefix scan, we skip inner values (they'd need
    // full key reconstruction). This is acceptable since command
    // palette entries always terminate at leaves.

That "acceptable since command palette entries always terminate at
leaves" caveat is no longer true — the command palette now accepts
entries where one command is a strict prefix of another, so the
silent skip was producing incomplete search results.

## Schema change

`ArtNode::Inner` gains a new `terminal_key: Option<String>` field
that is populated at exactly the two places where `value` is set
on an inner node:

- In `insert_recursive`, when a key terminates at a pre-existing
  inner node or when splitting a compressed prefix creates a new
  inner node whose split_depth lands on the key boundary. In that
  case both `inner_value = Some(value)` and
  `new_terminal_key = Some(full_key.to_string())` are set
  simultaneously so they cannot drift out of sync.
- In the split-existing-leaf path, where the pre-existing leaf's
  key is promoted to the new inner node if the split leaves it on
  the boundary. The old `key` is preserved in `terminal_key` so
  the original entry remains addressable.

`get_recursive` and `prefix_scan_recursive` are updated to
destructure `terminal_key: _` so they continue to compile — the
get path already reaches the value by walking the tree with the
full key in hand, and prefix_scan now defers to the (fixed)
`collect_all_inner`.

`collect_all_inner` now emits `(terminal_key, value)` when both
are set, before recursing into children. Result ordering is
unchanged for keys that only exist at leaves.

## Delete path

`delete_recursive` previously returned `Option<V>`, which carried
the removed value but no signal about whether the caller should
prune the empty parent. The top-level `delete` then had a fragile
"if the root looks empty, drop it" follow-up that couldn't detect
more interesting prune-upward cases.

`delete_recursive` now returns a `DeleteResult<V> { removed,
prune }` struct, with a `not_found()` constructor for the common
no-op case. The top-level `delete` uses the `prune` flag together
with the legacy root-is-empty check to drop the root when
appropriate, and restructures the `self.root` re-borrow so the
`result` value is materialized before `self.root` is touched
again, which avoids a double mutable borrow that the previous
code got away with only because it didn't need to read
`self.root` after calling `delete_recursive`.
